title: brickellia coahuilensis
intro: Brickellia coahuilensis, also known as Coahuilitea, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the family Asteraceae and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, pain relief, and improved immune function. Brickellia coahuilensis is typically prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been used for centuries in traditional medicine.
